evals.prompt: Initialize the swarm in an empty temp project directory. List every directory and key file you would create (do not write outside the sandbox if instructed; prefer reporting the plan with exact paths).
evals.expected_output: Full scaffold plan: .claude/agents/, .claude/commands/, .cursor/rules/, prd/, stories/, design/{hld/adrs,lld,ux}/, src/, web/, tests/, plus CLAUDE.md, eight agent defs, three slash commands, empty _queue.json, orchestrator.mdc.
expectations: Plan includes directories .claude/agents/, .claude/commands/, .cursor/rules/, prd/, stories/, design/hld/adrs/, design/lld/, design/ux/, src/, web/, tests/
Plan includes writing CLAUDE.md with the fullstack-swarm orchestrator constitution
Plan includes all eight subagent definitions (project-manager through visual-qa)
Plan includes slash commands ship-prd, status, and next

evals.prompt: Re-run /swarm-init on a project where CLAUDE.md and stories/_queue.json already exist. Do not pass --force. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: Skip existing files, note each skip, still create any missing scaffold pieces. No overwrite of CLAUDE.md or _queue.json.
expectations: Agent skips CLAUDE.md because it already exists and --force was not passed
Agent skips stories/_queue.json for the same reason
Agent notes skipped files rather than silently overwriting

evals.prompt: Run /swarm-init --force on a project that already has CLAUDE.md. What changes?
evals.expected_output: With --force, existing files are overwritten (including CLAUDE.md) rather than skipped.
expectations: Agent treats --force as permission to overwrite existing files
